topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the conference room felt thick, clinging to my skin like damp wool. I kept my eyes fixed on the polished mahogany table, meticulously straightening a stray crease in my notepad for the tenth time. Sarah, across from me, was laughing—a bright, unrestrained sound that seemed to vibrate in the silence I was desperately trying to manufacture. When Mr. Henderson finally stopped speaking, his gaze swept over us both, lingering for a fraction of a second too long on my slightly rumpled blazer. My palms grew suddenly slick. I could feel a distinct heat rising up my neck, a flush spreading across my cheeks that felt entirely too visible under the harsh fluorescent lights. I wanted the floor to swallow me whole.

"Well," Sarah said, leaning back with an air of practiced ease, "I hope they see my long-term vision more clearly than my... preliminary data." The small, sharp edge in her tone made my stomach clench. I cleared my throat, the sound surprisingly loud in the quiet room, and focused intently on the water glass in front of me, willing my breathing to become shallow and controlled.

[story 2]

I fiddled incessantly with the edge of my presentation clicker, the plastic cool against my clammy fingers. Every time the discussion turned toward the leadership track, a cold knot tightened in my chest, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. It was the way the light caught the sheen of sweat on my forehead, betraying the internal turmoil I was fighting to suppress. I watched Mark present his case—his delivery smooth, his confidence radiating outward like heat from an engine. When the opportunity for discussion arose, I found my voice caught in a dry rasp. "I... I believe my contributions in the Q3 restructuring are significant," I managed, the words coming out strained and too high-pitched. I could feel the scrutiny of the room, every eye assessing the tremor in my hands.

Mark offered a sympathetic nod, a gesture that felt patronizing. I quickly looked down at my notes, focusing on the meticulous handwriting, desperate to disappear into the ink. The silence that followed felt monumental, heavy with unspoken judgment. I risked a glance up, catching Sarah’s knowing smile, and a wave of profound awkwardness washed over me, making my throat constrict.

[story 3]

The moment the VP announced the dual candidates for the Senior Manager position, the world seemed to tilt slightly. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to become invisible, to simply dissolve into the beige carpet. My jaw tightened involuntarily, and I could feel the distinct pressure behind my eyes. I kept my posture rigid, a defensive shell against the sudden, intense spotlight. When it was my turn to speak, the words seemed to desert me. I opened my mouth, intending to articulate my qualifications clearly, but only a small, pathetic squeak escaped. I immediately regretted the sound, the way it echoed in the cavernous room. I could feel the blood draining from my face, leaving a strange, clammy sensation on my skin. I wished the floor would open up and consume the entirety of my current predicament.
